calendar.matters:
Petition For Amended Order And Reconsideration Of Order For Final Distibution On Waiver Of Accounting, And Allowing Executor'S And Attorneys' Compensation For Ordinary Services
Off Calendar Sep-28-2015 Advanced From Oct-28-15 To Oct-01-15 At 02:30 P.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Amended Order And Reconsideration Of Order For Final Distibution On Waiver Of Accounting, And Allowing Executor'S And Attorneys' Compensation For Ordinary Services
Off Calendar Oct-08-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Paul Melbostad for and with Thomas Larsen.

Mr. Melbostad reported that the issue is what Mr. Larsen received in advance, that it was only a portion of the listing agents commission, that he did not receive any portion of statutory compensation prior to court approval, that he did receive preliminary compensation with court approval, that they do not dispute that they should have gotten court approval prior to listing the property, that L.R. 14.95(c) is directed at statutory and extraordinary compensation only, that there is no dispute that he cannot seek extraordinary fees for preparing the property for sale, that they were able to sell the property for $900,000.00 more than the date of death appraisal, that they feel a surcharge or percentage penalty is not fair there could be a required payment of interest on commission from the date of payment to the present and that all of the beneficiaries were aware of what was taking place. Mr. Larsen reported that he had had many discussions with the decedent regarding the property, that he knew the beneficiaries, that he spent time and effort to obtain the value of the property, that time was spent with the beneficiaries trying to decide whether to keep the property or to sell and that he was not aware that court authorization was necessary. Mr. Melbostad stated that based on a discussion at a prior hearing they thought the court was aware that the property was being sold. The Court asked for a declaration regarding the payment. Mr. Larsen explained that the broker submits the commission demand to the escrow company and that one check was issued to Coldwell and one check to Mr. Larsen and that there is a document that shows what the company receive and what Mr. Larsen received. Mr. Melbostad stated that the examiner argues that the reason for prior approval is to obtain court approval to receive statutory compensation as well as real estate compensation instead of the primary purpose of court authorization being to keep an executor from unnecessarily selling property or being sold by an executor who is not qualified, that email notice was given to all beneficiaries regarding this hearing and that he spoke with Kathy and Dave Archibald. Mr. Larsen stated that they had explored the potential of getting approval for an addition and then selling the property with the plans and that Kathy Archibald had thought about buying the property but then decided against it. The Court stated that the rule is for transparency so that there is no doubt as to what is going on, requested that they file a declaration for clarification on what amount was actually received and took the petition for amended order and reconsideration of order for final distribution on waiver of accounting and allowing executor's and attorney's compensation for ordinary services under submission.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Allen. (Not Reported)

1) Advancing Hearing Date On Executor'S Petition For Amended Order And Reconsideration Of Order For Final Distribution On Waive Of Accounting, Etc., To 9/28/15, And 2) Dispensing With Notice Of Hearing Of The Advanced Hearing Date
Off Calendar Sep-28-2015 Granted Per Written Order

Motion For Amended Order And Reconsideration Of Order For Final Distribution On Waiver Of Accounting, And Allowing Executor'S And Attorneys' Compensation For Ordinary Services
Off Calendar Sep-09-2015 Text Ruling

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Jul-13-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Paul Melbostad, for and with petitioner Thomas Larsen.

Mr. Melbostad stated that he took full responsibility for not seeking prior Court approval for listing the property for sale through the agency of the executor. He explained that Mr. Larsen was the decedent's good friend, that the family members consented to him serving as broker on the sale, and that he had obtained an order authorizing the sale of the property. Mr. Larsen stated that he recommended that the beneficiaries not sell the property, but to keep it in the family if possible. Mr. Melbostad stated that the plan had been for Katherine Archibald to purchase the property, but that had not worked out so the property was placed on the market and sold. He stated that Mr. Larsen was willing to waive extraordinary fees. The Court took the matter under submission.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Craig Blackstone/Ella Yip. Bailiff: Deputy K. Kabasares. (Not Reported)

PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
Off Calendar Sep-09-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Paul Melbostad for and with Thomas Larsen.

Mr. Larson stated that the Beneficiaries agreed to a list price, that there are plans to list and market the property as early as next week and that no work was done to the home until the end of last year. Mr. Melbostad stated that they need a minimum of six months but would prefer nine months and that the Beneficiary that was interested in purchasing the home is no longer interested. The next probate accounting status report or petition for final distribution is to be filed by March 9, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy McConoco. (Not Reported)
